title: Remove Duplicates From Sorted List II
hints: Since the list is sorted, duplicates are always adjacent. Focus on how to detect and skip entire groups of identical values.
Use two pointers: one to track the last unique node, and another to scan ahead and find the end of any duplicate group.
Be careful with the head node itself; it might be part of a duplicate group, so consider using a dummy node before the head to simplify edge cases.
